passage.title: Plant Spot Trial
passage.text: Our garden club tested two places for young lettuce plants last month. Yuna used a bright open patch, but Theo used a cooler shady patch. After six days, Theo's plants looked less dry because the cooler patch held water longer. The club measured leaf size and wrote each change in a notebook. When Yuna saw the difference, she changed to a cooler shady patch. By the next check, her leaves were not curling as much. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that a cooler place can improve plant health.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Testing sun and shade for garden plants
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: Where did Theo put his young plants?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: The way two things are not the same
A loud sound in a room
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does shade mean in the passage?
choices: A person who visits a club
A place with less direct sunlight
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Yuna move her plants to a cooler shady patch?
choices: The group wanted to decorate the room more
The first tool was missing from the class
She thought the cooler patch would help the plants stay healthy
The teacher told the student to stop working
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
